prompt: Design a RAG pipeline for a legal document Q&A feature. Users will ask natural language questions against a corpus of 50,000 PDFs (contracts, litigation docs). Latency target: p99 < 2 seconds. The stack is Python with a PostgreSQL database already in place.
_note: Tests domain specificity for legal docs (section-aware chunking, citation retrieval) and concrete embedding/vector store decisions given the PostgreSQL constraint (pgvector vs. external). Latency budget test.
